trans-unit.source: The objective of this exercise is to get a feeling for geodata and start working with it. Understand the attribute table, sort it, select manually and export the selection, as well as load a basemap.
trans-unit.target: The objective of this exercise is to get a feeling for geodata and start working with it. Understand the attribute table, sort it, select manually and export the selection, as well as load a basemap.

trans-unit.source: This exercise can be used in online and presence training.
trans-unit.target: This exercise can be used in online and presence training.

trans-unit.source: It can be done as a follow-along exercise or individually as a self-study.
trans-unit.target: It can be done as a follow-along exercise or individually as a self-study.

trans-unit.source: Sorting and manipulating datasets through the attribute table
trans-unit.target: Sorting and manipulating datasets through the attribute table

trans-unit.source: performing basic and advanced spatial analyses
trans-unit.target: performing basic and advanced spatial analyses

trans-unit.source: The exercise takes around 1 hour to complete, depending on the number of participants and their familiarity with computer systems.
trans-unit.target: The exercise takes around 1 hour to complete, depending on the number of participants and their familiarity with computer systems.

trans-unit.source: Take the time to familiarise yourself with the exercise and the provided material.
trans-unit.target: Take the time to familiarise yourself with the exercise and the provided material.

trans-unit.source: Prepare a white-board. It can be either a physical whiteboard, a flip-chart, or a digital whiteboard (e.g. Miro board) where the participants can add their findings and questions.
trans-unit.target: Prepare a white-board. It can be either a physical whiteboard, a flip-chart, or a digital whiteboard (e.g. Miro board) where the participants can add their findings and questions.

trans-unit.source: Before starting the exercise, make sure everybody has installed QGIS and has downloaded __and unzipped__ the data folder.
trans-unit.target: Before starting the exercise, make sure everybody has installed QGIS and has downloaded __and unzipped__ the data folder.

trans-unit.source: Check out [How to do trainings?](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Trainers_corner/en_how_to_training.html#how-to-do-trainings) for some general tips on training conduction
trans-unit.target: Check out [How to do trainings?](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Trainers_corner/en_how_to_training.html#how-to-do-trainings) for some general tips on training conduction

trans-unit.source: Introduce the idea and aim of the exercise.
trans-unit.target: Introduce the idea and aim of the exercise.

trans-unit.source: Provide the download link and make sure everybody has unzipped the folder before beginning the tasks.
trans-unit.target: Provide the download link and make sure everybody has unzipped the folder before beginning the tasks.

trans-unit.source: Show and explain each step yourself at least twice and slow enough so everybody can see what you are doing, and follow along in their own QGIS-project.
trans-unit.target: Show and explain each step yourself at least twice and slow enough so everybody can see what you are doing, and follow along in their own QGIS-project.

trans-unit.source: Make sure that everybody is following along and doing the steps themselves by periodically asking if anybody needs help or if everybody is still following.
trans-unit.target: Make sure that everybody is following along and doing the steps themselves by periodically asking if anybody needs help or if everybody is still following.

trans-unit.source: Be open and patient to every question or problem that might come up. Your participants are essentially multitasking by paying attention to your instructions and orienting themselves in their own QGIS-project.
trans-unit.target: Be open and patient to every question or problem that might come up. Your participants are essentially multitasking by paying attention to your instructions and orienting themselves in their own QGIS-project.

trans-unit.source: Leave time for any issues or questions concerning the tasks at the end of the exercise.
trans-unit.target: Leave time for any issues or questions concerning the tasks at the end of the exercise.

trans-unit.source: Leave some time for open questions.
trans-unit.target: Leave some time for open questions.

trans-unit.source: __Download all datasets [here](https://nexus.heigit.org/repository/gis-training-resource-center/Module_2/Exercise_2/Module_2_Exercise_2_Basic_geodata_processing.zip), save the folder on your computer and unzip the file.__
trans-unit.target: __Download all datasets [here](https://nexus.heigit.org/repository/gis-training-resource-center/Module_2/Exercise_2/Module_2_Exercise_2_Basic_geodata_processing.zip), save the folder on your computer and unzip the file.__

trans-unit.source: The shapefile for populated places contains __point data__ on human settlements in Nigeria, including cities, villages and others. The GeoJSON file for the boundaries of Nigeria contains information on the administrative boundaries at levels 2 and 4 with level 2 representing the whole country and level 4 being the states.
trans-unit.target: The shapefile for populated places contains __point data__ on human settlements in Nigeria, including cities, villages and others. The GeoJSON file for the boundaries of Nigeria contains information on the administrative boundaries at levels 2 and 4 with level 2 representing the whole country and level 4 being the states.

trans-unit.source: GeoJSON does not support multiple layers, so the polygons for the country boundaries and the states are merged into one layer __where the different polygons overlap__.
trans-unit.target: GeoJSON does not support multiple layers, so the polygons for the country boundaries and the states are merged into one layer __where the different polygons overlap__.

trans-unit.source: Add the OpenStreetMap basemap via the browser panel → `XYZ Tiles`.
trans-unit.target: Add the OpenStreetMap basemap via the browser panel → `XYZ Tiles`.

trans-unit.source: Familiarise yourself with the data by opening the attribute table. Determine the location of the data and the type of information it contains. Understand the different columns and the data they contain in your attribute table and try to get an overview of which columns will be relevant and important for your analysis.
trans-unit.target: Familiarise yourself with the data by opening the attribute table. Determine the location of the data and the type of information it contains. Understand the different columns and the data they contain in your attribute table and try to get an overview of which columns will be relevant and important for your analysis.

trans-unit.source: In the `nigeria_populated_places` layer, open the attribute table, select the feature for **Zuyel**, and zoom to the selected point.
trans-unit.target: In the `nigeria_populated_places` layer, open the attribute table, select the feature for **Zuyel**, and zoom to the selected point.

trans-unit.source: :::{Hint} The place starts with a *Z* so it might help to sort the `name` column in descending order. :::
trans-unit.target: :::{Hint} The place starts with a *Z* so it might help to sort the `name` column in descending order. :::

trans-unit.source: Export **Zuyel** as its own GeoPackage. Check the projection and choose an appropriate CRS. Name it `zuyel.gpkg`.
trans-unit.target: Export **Zuyel** as its own GeoPackage. Check the projection and choose an appropriate CRS. Name it `zuyel.gpkg`.

trans-unit.source: Repeat the steps for the layer `nigeria_boundaries.geojson` and only export the district in which **Zuyel** is located. Name it accordingly. To find the district use the ![](/fig/qgis_identify_features.png) `Identify Features` tool and then manually select the correct district in the attribute table.
trans-unit.target: Repeat the steps for the layer `nigeria_boundaries.geojson` and only export the district in which **Zuyel** is located. Name it accordingly. To find the district use the ![](/fig/qgis_identify_features.png) `Identify Features` tool and then manually select the correct district in the attribute table.

trans-unit.source: Remove all the initial layers and then open the attribute table for each of your new layers and check that each layer only contains one feature.
trans-unit.target: Remove all the initial layers and then open the attribute table for each of your new layers and check that each layer only contains one feature.

trans-unit.source: This is how your output could look like in the end :::
trans-unit.target: This is how your output could look like in the end :::
